schema.name: openauditbrasil.github_projects_automation_spec
purpose: Serve as a machine-readable blueprint to (1) create/configure GitHub Projects (v2) for the OpenAudit Brasil organization and (2) seed the project with initial work items aligned with the official docs.
Provide a single source of truth for contributors: mission, boundaries, risk controls, methodology, architecture, and roadmap.

notes: Preferred for CI automation and least-privilege setups.
Ensure the app has Projects permissions and, if linking a repository during project creation, repository Contents permission as required by GitHub.
Simpler to bootstrap locally; rotate and store securely.

documents.title: Roadmap — Proposta Técnica de Arquitetura e Requisitos

project_overview.name: OpenAudit Brasil
project_overview.tagline: Infraestrutura aberta para análise responsável, reprodutível e auditável de dados públicos
project_overview.core_goal: Build open, auditable infrastructure that produces explainable technical indicators from officially published public datasets, with strict risk controls and no accusatory outputs.
what_it_is: A technical infrastructure for structured public data analysis
A reproducible pipeline: source → snapshot → transform → quality → indicators → report → audit trail
A framework for deterministic indicators (anomaly / divergence / inconsistency) with explicit limitations
Optionally, an AI plug-in layer that outputs hypotheses (never conclusions) and must reference evidence already present in the report
what_it_is_not: A tool to accuse or label people

phases.name: Fundamentos (documentação e governança)
deliverables: Docs normativos publicados e versionados (manifesto/metodologia/risco/governança/segurança/código de conduta)
Templates e labels para issues/PRs com risco
definition_of_done: Fluxo de RFC e DECISIONS ativo

phases.name: MVP Search + Entity Catalog (sem cruzamento)
deliverables: UI/CLI para busca → lista de candidatos (confidence + explanation)
definition_of_done: Busca retorna lista de candidatos; não auto-seleciona

phases.name: MVP Analyze (1–2 fontes) + relatório auditável
definition_of_done: Sem linguagem acusatória e sem PII por padrão

phases.name: Camada IA plugável (multi-agente) — hipóteses assistidas
deliverables: Policy guard (bloquear linguagem proibida e PII)
Auditoria completa da IA (hashes, versões, prompts/policies)
definition_of_done: IA só gera hipóteses com evidence_refs

phases.name: Escalar conectores + qualidade + observabilidade
definition_of_done: Conectores com rate limit/backoff e source records

phases.name: Entity Resolution avançado (quando necessário)
deliverables: UI de desambiguação avançada (apenas quando necessário)
definition_of_done: Redução mensurável de falso positivo em ER

phases.name: Export controlado e pacote auditável
definition_of_done: Compartilhamento não vira ferramenta de perseguição

projects_to_manage.title: OpenAudit Brasil — Roadmap
projects_to_manage.short_description: Roadmap e triagem de contribuições do OpenAudit Brasil (projeto técnico, neutro e auditável).
projects_to_manage.long_description_markdown:
Infraestrutura aberta para análise responsável, reprodutível e auditável de dados públicos.

**Regras de ouro:** não acusa, não julga, não facilita perseguição. Resultados são *indicadores técnicos* com limitações explícitas.

Docs de referência:
- README.md (visão geral)
- ROADMAP.md (arquitetura e requisitos)
- MANIFESTO.md / METHODOLOGY.md / RISK_POLICY.md / GOVERNANCE.md / CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md / SECURITY.md

Como usar este projeto:
1) Abra uma issue com contexto, motivação e risco
2) Use labels e campos do Project (Risk/Area/Type)
3) Mudanças sensíveis exigem RFC + risk-review + registro em DECISIONS.md

built_in.name: Status
built_in.notes: Used by board columns; default options typically include Todo/In Progress/Done.

custom.name: Phase
custom.notes: Align items with ROADMAP phases. Useful for Roadmap (timeline) grouping.

custom.name: OA Workflow
custom.notes: Recommended grouping field for a custom Board view (manual view setup may be needed).

custom.name: Effort (pts)
custom.notes: Optional. Use for capacity planning and field sums in grouped views.

custom.name: Docs refs
custom.notes: Store comma-separated doc_ids or links; keeps item self-contained for reviewers.

recommended_views.name: Roadmap (Timeline)
recommended_views.notes: Requires an additional field 'Phase' (single_select) if you want grouping; otherwise group by Area or Risk.

known_api_limitations: Project views (table/board/roadmap) creation/editing may not be fully supported via API; plan for a one-time manual setup and keep the desired config here as documentation for humans/automation tooling.
The built-in Status field option management (create/edit/delete options) is limited; automation should prefer custom single-select fields (e.g., OA Workflow) for stable workflows.

graphql.query: mutation CreateProject($input:CreateProjectV2Input!){ createProjectV2(input:$input){ projectV2{ id number title } } }
notes: If the project already exists, query and reuse it instead of recreating.

bootstrap_steps.name: Discover field IDs and option IDs

graphql.query: query GetProjectFields($org:String!, $number:Int!){ organization(login:$org){ projectV2(number:$number){ id title fields(first:50){ nodes{ ... on ProjectV2FieldCommon{ id name dataType } ... on ProjectV2SingleSelectField{ id name options{ id name } } } } } } }

notes: Use createProjectV2Field* mutations (text/number/date/single-select/iteration) when available; otherwise mark as manual.
When creating single-select fields, store resulting option IDs.

bootstrap_steps.name: Seed project with initial items (draft issues or linked issues)
Set field values for each project item

notes: Use a workflow to sync issues/PRs into the org project and keep fields updated.
Useful locally for bootstrapping and debugging GraphQL calls.

rfc_required_when: new indicator or changes to indicator thresholds
any output that may be interpreted as accusatory
risk_review_required_when: PII/identifiers involved (even if in public sources)
any feature that increases targeting/doxxing risk

pr_template_minimum_checklist: No PII in logs, fixtures, examples (masked if unavoidable)
Indicators include evidence + limitations + alt hypotheses + confidence
run_record/audit fields updated if pipeline behavior changes
If risk:high: includes risk mitigation + rollback plan + DECISIONS entry

core_components.name: Search Engine
core_components.responsibility: Free-text search → candidate entities list with match confidence/explanation

core_components.name: Analyze Orchestrator
core_components.responsibility: Coordinates phase-2 pipeline; enforces cache, risk guards, audit

core_components.name: Connectors
core_components.responsibility: Collect raw snapshots from approved sources with rate limit/backoff

core_components.name: Transformers
core_components.responsibility: Map raw snapshots → canonical schema datasets (versioned)

core_components.name: Quality Gate
core_components.responsibility: Data quality checks; produces QualityReport and affects confidence

core_components.name: Deterministic Indicators Engine
core_components.responsibility: Compute explainable indicators (deterministic) with evidence and limitations

core_components.name: AI Agent Orchestrator (optional)
core_components.responsibility: Run N agents; aggregate consensus/divergence; enforce policy guard

core_components.name: Report Builder
core_components.responsibility: Produce structured report JSON with disclaimers, sources, and audit section

core_components.name: Observability
core_components.responsibility: Structured logs (no PII), metrics, traces with run_id

outputs: RawSnapshot{snapshot_id, source, collected_at, uri, hash, payload_ref}

hard_rules: Agent must not introduce new facts

required_disclaimers.block_A_non_accusation: Este resultado é um indicador técnico. Não constitui prova, denúncia, acusação ou afirmação de irregularidade.
required_disclaimers.block_B_limitations: Resultados dependem da qualidade/completude dos dados e podem conter falsos positivos.
required_disclaimers.block_C_methodology: Metodologia e critérios: ver METHODOLOGY.md (versão vigente).

absolute_prohibitions: rankings that imply wrongdoing (e.g., 'top suspects', 'fraud score')
scraping/bypass of login/captcha/session or ToS-evasive collection
hosting/distributing default datasets with identifiable personal data

templates.issue_template_minimum_markdown:
## Contexto
- O que você quer mudar/criar? Por quê?

## Proposta
- Descreva a mudança, incluindo alternativas consideradas.

## Risco (obrigatório)
- Nível: risk:low | risk:medium | risk:high
- Envolve PII/identificadores? (sim/não) Explique.
- Pode ser interpretado como acusação? (sim/não) Explique.
- Pode facilitar targeting/doxxing? (sim/não) Explique.
- ToS/licença da fonte é compatível? (sim/não) Link.

## Referências
- Docs: README / ROADMAP / METHODOLOGY / RISK_POLICY / GOVERNANCE

## Critérios de aceite
- [ ] Testes (ou justificativa)
- [ ] Output explicável (evidência + limitações + alt hipóteses)
- [ ] Sem linguagem acusatória
- [ ] Sem PII por padrão
templates.pr_template_minimum_markdown:
## O que mudou

## Por que mudou

## Risco
- Nível (risk:low|medium|high): 
- `risk-review:required` aplicado? (sim/não)

## Checklist
- [ ] Sem linguagem acusatória (código/docs/UI/outputs)
- [ ] Sem PII em logs/fixtures/exemplos (ou mascarado)
- [ ] Indicadores com evidência + limitações + alt hipóteses + confiança
- [ ] Testes adicionados/atualizados (ou justificativa)
- [ ] Documentação atualizada (quando aplicável)
- [ ] Se risk:high: RFC + DECISIONS.md + plano de rollback

seed_items.notes: Items are designed to be created as draft issues in the org Project, then converted to real issues per-repo when implementation starts.

items.title: Bootstrap GitHub Project: fields, labels, templates, and branch protection
acceptance_criteria: Org project has required custom fields (OA Workflow, Risk, Type, Area, Priority, etc.)
Repos adopt branch protection + mandatory CI for main
Issue/PR templates exist and enforce risk checklist
Labels catalog is created consistently across repos
deliverables: Documented automation workflow (GitHub Actions) for syncing items
items.body_markdown:
**Key:** T-0001
**Phase:** PH-00
**Risk:** risk:medium

## Objetivo
Bootstrap GitHub Project: fields, labels, templates, and branch protection

## Critérios de aceite
- [ ] Org project has required custom fields (OA Workflow, Risk, Type, Area, Priority, etc.)
- [ ] Repos adopt branch protection + mandatory CI for main
- [ ] Issue/PR templates exist and enforce risk checklist
- [ ] Labels catalog is created consistently across repos

## Entregáveis
- .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/*
- .github/pull_request_template.md
- CODEOWNERS (when repos exist)
- Documented automation workflow (GitHub Actions) for syncing items

## Referências (docs)
GOVERNANCE, RISK_POLICY, SECURITY, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, ROADMAP

items.title: Define canonical entity_id strategy and initial catalog source (RFC)
acceptance_criteria: RFC defines entity_id format, alias strategy, and disambiguation rules
Includes risk analysis for homonyms, targeting, and PII handling
Defines what fields are allowed in catalog output (conservative defaults)
items.body_markdown:
**Key:** T-0101
**Phase:** PH-01
**Risk:** risk:high

## Objetivo
Define canonical entity_id strategy and initial catalog source (RFC)

## Critérios de aceite
- [ ] RFC defines entity_id format, alias strategy, and disambiguation rules
- [ ] Includes risk analysis for homonyms, targeting, and PII handling
- [ ] Defines what fields are allowed in catalog output (conservative defaults)

## Entregáveis
- RFC document + DECISIONS entry
- Initial catalog schema (versioned)

## Referências (docs)
ROADMAP, METHODOLOGY, RISK_POLICY, GOVERNANCE

items.title: Implement run_record + audit schema (deterministic pipeline tracking)
acceptance_criteria: Each pipeline run produces run_id and run_record with code_version, connector versions, dataset fingerprints, quality checks, indicators IDs/params
No PII in audit/logs by default (masking rules applied)
items.body_markdown:
**Key:** T-0201
**Phase:** PH-02
**Risk:** risk:medium

## Objetivo
Implement run_record + audit schema (deterministic pipeline tracking)

## Critérios de aceite
- [ ] Each pipeline run produces run_id and run_record with code_version, connector versions, dataset fingerprints, quality checks, indicators IDs/params
- [ ] No PII in audit/logs by default (masking rules applied)

- Audit Store interface

## Referências (docs)
ROADMAP, METHODOLOGY, RISK_POLICY, SECURITY

items.title: MVP Analyze pipeline (1 source): snapshot -> normalize -> quality -> 1 indicator -> report JSON
acceptance_criteria: Processing starts only after explicit entity selection
Indicator is deterministic, explainable, lists false positives and alt hypotheses
items.body_markdown:
**Key:** T-0202
**Phase:** PH-02
**Risk:** risk:medium

## Objetivo
MVP Analyze pipeline (1 source): snapshot -> normalize -> quality -> 1 indicator -> report JSON

## Critérios de aceite
- [ ] Processing starts only after explicit entity selection
- [ ] Report includes summary/entities/facts/indicators/sources/audit
- [ ] Indicator is deterministic, explainable, lists false positives and alt hypotheses
- [ ] Cache_hit and TTL recorded

## Entregáveis
- core-engine skeleton
- 1 connector + transformer
- 1 indicator spec + tests

## Referências (docs)
ROADMAP, METHODOLOGY, RISK_POLICY

items.title: AI plug-in interface + policy guard + ai audit fields (no facts, only hypotheses)
acceptance_criteria: AiAgent only outputs findings with evidence_refs into existing facts/indicators/sources
audit.ai[] includes model_id, model_version, prompt_version, policy_version, input_hash, output_hash, duration_ms, nondeterministic flag
Policy guard blocks banned terms and PII in AI outputs by default
items.body_markdown:
**Key:** T-0301
**Phase:** PH-03
**Risk:** risk:high

## Objetivo
AI plug-in interface + policy guard + ai audit fields (no facts, only hypotheses)

## Critérios de aceite
- [ ] AiAgent only outputs findings with evidence_refs into existing facts/indicators/sources
- [ ] audit.ai[] includes model_id, model_version, prompt_version, policy_version, input_hash, output_hash, duration_ms, nondeterministic flag
- [ ] Policy guard blocks banned terms and PII in AI outputs by default

## Entregáveis
- AiAgent interface + orchestrator
- policy guard rules
- RFC + DECISIONS entry

## Referências (docs)
ROADMAP, METHODOLOGY, RISK_POLICY, SECURITY, GOVERNANCE
